title: Burail Fort
tagline: Burail Fort — heritage city of Chandigarh

overview.short: Burail Fort is a fort situated in Sector 45 of Chandigarh, India (presently).It Is firstly Constructed by Raghuvanshi Rajputs in 12th Century.
overview.description: Burail Fort is a fort situated in Sector 45 of Chandigarh, India (presently).It Is firstly Constructed by Raghuvanshi Rajputs in 12th Century. It was built during the Mughal period.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Terraced Garden, Chandigarh
topPlaces.description: The Terraced Garden is a public garden in the city of Chandigarh, India. Established in 1979, it spans an area of approximately 10 acres (4.0 hectares) and is known for its distinctive terraced layout.

topPlaces.name: Model Jail, Chandigarh
topPlaces.description: Model Jail is a prison located in the Union Territory of Chandigarh, India. It is a Central Jail established in 1972.

topPlaces.name: Japanese Garden, Chandigarh
topPlaces.description: The Japanese Garden is a park located in Sector 31 in the Union Territory of Chandigarh. Built in 2014 on 13 acres of land by the Indian Government, it was inaugurated by Shivraj Patil on 7 November 2014.

topPlaces.name: Parrot Bird Sanctuary, Chandigarh
topPlaces.description: City Bird Sanctuary Chandigarh or Parrot Bird Sanctuary Chandigarh is a bird sanctuary situated in sector 21 of the city of Chandigarh, India. It is primarily habitat of parrots.

topPlaces.name: Sanitary Garden
topPlaces.description: The Sanitary Garden or Kala Sagar (lit. 'ocean of art') is an art exhibition in Chandigarh, India consisting of sculptures made from toilets and other sanitary items. The garden is a unique government museum where the world's largest collection of art created purely from damaged or discarded sanitary items is exhibited.

topPlaces.name: Chandigarh Hockey Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Chandigarh Hockey Stadium is a field hockey stadium at Chandigarh, India. It was the home of the Chandigarh Dynamos of the Premier Hockey League.

topPlaces.name: Phase 9, Mohali
topPlaces.description: Sector 63 (popularly known as Phase 9) is an important locality in Mohali famous for sports as Punjab Cricket Association Stadium, International Hockey Stadium and Sports complex of Department of Sports is situated in this locality. The seasonal rivulet N Choe (also called Attawa Choa) passes through it.

topPlaces.name: I. S. Bindra Stadium
topPlaces.description: The I. S. Bindra Punjab Cricket Stadium is a cricket ground located in Mohali, Punjab, India. It is commonly referred to as the Mohali Stadium.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Burail Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Burail Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Burail Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chandigarh International Airport (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (6 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Burail Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Burail Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Burail Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Burail Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Burail Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Terraced Garden, Chandigarh, Model Jail, Chandigarh, Japanese Garden, Chandigarh, Parrot Bird Sanctuary, Chandigarh, Sanitary Garden.
